reviews.review_text:
I used to play this game all the time, but I just cannot bring myself to play it much ever since the buyout was announced.  

For those of you who don't know, EA is getting bought by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund (PIF) and Jared Kushner’s Affinity Partners.  PIF is owned by THE Saudi Arabian Government, which is known for committing human rights abuses.

The game WAS fun but I will never buy an EA product again with these new owners profiting from it, for I am not comfortable with any of my purchases supporting such heinous crimes!  I urge YOU to follow suit!
$1600 dollars for everything out right now on here. Thanks EA for being one of the most predatory, anti-creative, and downright corrupt gaming companies to date.

The greediest and most money grubbing game of all time - as of writing $1,596.89 in downloadable content - exact.

An unprecedented level of scam. Past Sims games charged NOTHING to have basic gameplay features be available to players.

Maxis lost the plot years ago. EA smiles as you empty your pockets into their Swiss bank account

'Play for free' but it takes a bit of my sanity away every time I open this game. What started as a very cool game, ends like a biggest disappointment to me. I have over 1k hours spent on building and playing, and I always end up losing the complete save due to bugs (even without mods). And yet, EA is still tossing around new packs and DLCs without fixing the age old existing bugs that prevent players from playing normally. 

Let me ask a question. If you keep expanding your game with new worlds and places for sims to visit, one can conclude that it has to be at least playable right? If I want to create a world with every place occupied, that should be doable, right? Well, sorry to disappoint, but after you spend hours in building and creating, the file corrupts and is not usable, all because the game can't handle it. THEN WHY GIVE US SO MANY DLCS. Also, before anyone starts blaming my game equipment, it runs games like baldurs gate 3 and spiderman fine. Not the issue. 

Man, I used to be happy to play the sims. Now I try and be disappointed to see my game STILL doesn't work. IT HAS BEEN 12 YEARS SINCE RELEASED. EA is the biggest money farmer I know. Shame.

This is the worst The Sims ever made, even worst than Sims 3 (that was already a disaster).
The graphics are so poor, sims 2 textures and polygons was far more beautiful than the Sims 4. To me, it's a confession from Maxis and the engine they uses is defintly bad.
The Sims 3 was so deeply bugged that it was a deception for all players, they decided to go back and return to a low definition Sims 2, the content has been wiped again and everything is sold separatly.

This sales model is ridiculous, it's a scam, they remove all contents from their previous game every time they release a new version of their game, and they charge full prices for concept and assets that you already bought in the past. This would not be so bad if at least, the game was better than the previous, but here, the quality are going down every generation.

Don't give your money to such a greedy company.
Expensive and bloated. Coasting on nostalgia. Love the game, but it's like stockholm syndrome; would never recommend this to people because someone has to break the cycle.

I'd rather fall down a flight of stairs than even look at this game again.

Their game ruining updates render it unplayable, break mods, etc..
Every time you visit a forum with a problem, they scapegoat mods.
And the latest weekly challenge is to just die a bunch? During June? REALLY? Way to worsen the widespread /ideation/.

0/10 devs. They ruined a beloved series with greed.
As a new gamer, you only need to pay €1,596.89 for all the additional content to play the full game.
99% of the content is locked behind a paywall, which doesn't even matter because the base gameplay experience is abysmal ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

I recommend the game not the developers:

I think this is a game you have to try, However i also think who the creators of a game are willing to sell their games to speaks volumes about them.

And EA are now selling a large chunk of their company to a what is essentially the saudi arabian goverment, Make of that what you will.

Other notable issues:
- Every update seems to introduce new gamebreaking bugs
- Long standing bugs aren't fixed
- Player feedback, suggestions, and bug reports are ignored
- New content keeps shrinking while the price stays the same
- To play the entire game you have to spend thousands of dollars(DLC)

So many years have passed, yer EA still dont give a dam about this game. Lack of fetures that have been in Sims 2 and 3,  many bugs, DLCs that give literaly nothing yet cost as much as the base game did.

Overpriced DLCs, glitches as soon as you sneeze at the wrong time.
If you're on Linux and want to stay on a fixed version (as to not risk breaking all your mods with every update, even if said update just adds customisation options for stairs), it's a nightmare to get to work with Proton due to the EA Desktop App's constant demand for updates. I've probably spent more time debugging this accursed setup than actually playing the game. Even EasyAntiCheat runs better with current Proton than the flaky mess of (what appears to be) an Electron app that is the EA Desktop App.
If you play without mods, are on Windows and like spending copious amounts of money for a slow, buggy amalgamation of DLCs of wildly varying quality, this is the game for you.

tl;dr not worth it, interesting stuff locked behind DLCs, save your money
1к$ for dlc...well, I'd like to do that, but I just better max guitar for half a life straight for free.

It's an admittedly entertaining time-suck, but (even ignoring the buy-out issues) unless EA changes their predatory practice of any and all new content being an expensive new purchase, I cannot in good conscience recommend this game.

since the early 2010s, i’ve been part of this scene. it started with playing the sims freeplay up to level 30 on my dad's samsung tab 2. i was obsessed with the time-consuming limited-time event rewards and packing my houses full of them. when i found out a full pc version existed, i spent ages looking for a way to play it for free, but had no luck and stuck to freeplay. eventually, as a kid with no pocket money, i downloaded a pirated copy just to taste what it was like. 

this game honestly helped shape who i am. it made me swallow my pride, bring my head down from the clouds, and finally come out of my shell. as someone who deals with severe anxiety, it pushed me to socialise and express myself in ways i couldn't in the real world. it also completely birthed my deep investment into the simulation genre and its history. 

growing up, i didn't actually play with physical dollhouses, but i'd see my friends with them or spot them in tv ads and absolutely drool over wanting to build one. for years as a little kid, i would play with building blocks shaped like houses and build house fortresses out of cushions. i constantly dreamt of playing pretend with a house. i even bunked a class in grade 5 just to roam the school grounds under a palm tree, improvising a home in my head. this game just ticked all my boxes. it became the ultimate digital sandbox of that childhood dream, letting me build, design, and control everything. 

the soundtrack was absolutely iconic and kept me hooked for hours. on top of that, modding is incredibly seamless. i even made a small mod myself back in 2021 and was amazed by how accessible the tools were.

the hours you see on my steam profile (around 11 hours at the time of writing) don't show the real picture. i've been playing this game day in, day out since 2017, letting my imagination run completely wild. 

the only reason this game went from holding a "special place" in my heart to holding a "special place in the past" is corporate greed. when a game requires nearly $1,000 usd in dlcs just to feel complete, it completely kills the enthusiasm. ea literally milked the player base dry before finally making the base game free-to-play. while i've ditched piracy now that i can support games properly, companies that corporate-greed their products this hard almost invite it. the constant churning out of low-effort dlcs has caused my enthusiasm to decline, but i still hold onto a bit of hope that they can turn things around and bring back the magic this franchise used to have.

Grew up loving the sims franchise. I tried so hard to love sims 4 I have over 300 hours, bought at least half the DLC hoping things would change. This company sucks constantly releasing new dlc that would completely break your game every release should be unacceptable. I am so happy I can now say goodbye to sims 4 since ive gotten Paralives. EA you f'd around and found out and now an indie team is showing everyone how despicable you really are.

as someone who has been playing long before this game went Free to play i loved this game but i honestly cant anymore the greed has gone to EA's head if i were to buy all of the dlcs id have to drop over 1000 dollars witch is insane and to make matters worse the price has gone up because of these "kits" 5 or 6 pieces of Furniture at best the add more and more with out fixing any of the pre existing bugs EA doesnt care anymore all the sims is is a cash crop that as far as they can tell wont dry up and whats worse people keep buying these kits and those half baked Expansions honestly im done i cant play this game anymore i just cant

with new competition out u realise this is a piece of ♥♥♥♥ game with basically the same amount of built in content as a early access entry. they only made the base game free bc they make money on their dlcs being horribly built and almost impossible to refund.

Re-installed the game after a brief bit of nostalgia and was immediately barraged by advertisements for things I didn't have. After fixing all of my mods to the newest version, I tried to play, only for a floating icon advertising the Bridgerton collection and a popup telling me to rule as a noble appear on my screen. I closed and uninstalled. 

The Sims 4 has a good game in it, but it's buried under predatory transactions and advertisements and the actual gameplay tends to be shockingly dull. I doubt even the devs are enjoying working on it these days.
Honestly really fun and relaxing. I like how you can create your own characters and stories however you want. Building houses is super addictive too. Definitely one of my comfort games.

i have paid about 80 euros for this game and its dlc and i got something that is worth under 30 euros, dlc does not bring new interesting content, usually its just over-repeated over-used content from previsous packs, base game is plain. Only reason why i had play so many hours is the custom content made by fans of this broken game, which MAXIS is very unfriendly to. As they let us know after each update after turning setting of our game to not be able to use custom content ,,the sims 4 do not support custom content", so we have to restart this game. Also they dont have any platform they would make using custom content more comfortable (like steam workshop), that puts many simmers in dangerous situation like in risk downoloding virus in their computer.  the marketplace is one of the most horrible things i had seen, buying tshirt for your sim would be early more expensive  than buying tshirt in real life. I will not spent any more money before they make this game worth the money i had already spent and I call for everybody do the same.

So disappointed at the recent changes sims has underwent, making the game free is so absurd to me because millions of players like me have had to purchase the game in the very beginning to play it, why punish your longest customers? Also increasing the price of packs to where it's like basically buying a new game for the price of one expansion pack, half of the packs or additions you guys add should have been implemented in base game with no charge. 
 The market place has only created sims to appear as pay to win, or pay to enjoy. Players lose so much access to their previous modes and also have to face the consequences of when a company like EA chooses to be capitalistic over realistic with their players money.  I used to enjoy the sims a lot but these recent changes have made me realize that they are moving in such a money hungry focused way I can't enjoy the game anymore. Safe to say I'm not touching this game again

i have been a sims fan for a long time but i can no longer suggest this to people the developer has become super lazy and money hungry sadly there is enough content to make a 5th game but prob wont happen the only reason they made this game free ways to JUSTIIFY shoveling ♥♥♥♥ DLC for money and 3 quarters of should just be in the game dont support there crap DLC prices they even had a co-op sims one The sims busting out but after sims 3 every sims will sadly prob be a FAT CASH COW

For a game that has been ongoing for so long, the bugs are ridiculous. Constant DLC that creates more bugs and glitches. The cost of said DLC being absolutely mind boggling considering the lack of content in them. This game needs more competition and dethroned as THE life simulator. The amount of money I have sank into this game for it to barely function is stupid. If you haven't been caught in this trap. Don't start.

EA doesn't care about anything the people want. They only care about the money. We shouldn't have too mod nearly every aspect of the game just to make it fun and we shouldn't have to ay hundreds of dollars for features that should be base game.
Very good and fun to build in, def recommend mods if you cant afford getting the official expansion and build packs!
The most buggy, flamey, piece of overpriced, expensive, greedy trash you'll ever own.
Obviously Sims is one of the best simluation games out there! I always come back to this game time and time again. It will never get old for me and you can be so creative with it.

The game has pretty great graphics for a Sims game. I've been playing the Sims franchise since 2008, and I like the graphics of Sims 4 best, gameplay and storylines of Sims 3 best, and Sims 3 wins for color picker and decoration and customization- however, you can download Sims Studio and create and customize items for Sims 4, so it's not so bad in that aspect. I wish the DLC packs weren't so expensive and that the company cared more about players and less about how much money they can get out of us. I wish the DLC packs were less buggy and didn't break the game when you have them all loaded in there along with custom content- but that might also be a device issue. I run Sims 4 mostly on my laptop and occasionally on my PC. Neither of which are the top models with top specs.
its alright, it costs a lot to get the most out of it and modding can be a pain due to all the updates but its fun once you get past all that
I LOVE the sims 4 i think it´s a good way to try new aesthetics, build cute homes and try fun challenges

it can be extremely buggy and the game's monetization is fairly dystopian. but if you catch some of the content packs on a good sale (or just install custom content) and learn how to avoid certain bugs, it can be an addictively fun life/dollhouse/dumb reality show simulator. the game is pretty open ended, though, so being creative with how you design your sims' lives/stories can make it more fun, but playing aimlessly can detract from the experience sometimes. i can't really recommend buying any content at retail price, but it does go on sale pretty frequently.
The game randomly crashed and I lost all my game progress. It doesn't even support auto-saving like any other game.

If you plan on playing this game without mods then go for it it's pretty good. But if your like me and have mods from years ago that don't get updated anymore then don't bother. With the constant updates that come out they break the game so much that it's actually unplayable for me and my mods don't get updated anymore. It's sad that theirs no downgrader for this game because I would go back and play it if I could just downgrade it to a few patches ago

this game is so good! I dislike how long it takes to load certain things but other than that I would so recommend this to new players or people who are looking for a good game. it has a mix of roleplay, building  and customising people {sims}. I hope if your getting this you will love it!
I've been playing The Sims since I was little. This is an amazing game! However, the expansion packs make up most of the game, but overall, it's worth it
I have thousands of hours in the sims, have played religiously for over 20 years. Sims 4 is a bit lackluster compared to the other games in terms of chaos happening but with mods, it's good.

You cannot enjoy this game properly without buying like $100 worth of extra content. I tried to play without my FOUR DLCs and it felt bland. The only thing that makes up for it is the mods. It's free so you can try it, but don't fall for the trap of "Just buy one DLC" because then you will want to buy more and it adds up FAST. MODS ARE AMAZING THOUGH!
this game is very fun it so fun to create character and build

Overall, The Sims 3 was the better Sims game, but sadly it feels pretty outdated nowadays, so we’re stuck with The Sims 4 — which, to be fair, isn’t a complete letdown.

Mods definitely extend the game’s lifespan, and there are a ton of great ones ranging from simple cosmetics to major gameplay overhauls.
Yes, even *those* kinds of mods. Highly recommended. ;D

What *is* a complete letdown, though, is the DLC policy. It’s absolutely ridiculous — greed at its finest. GreedSLOP, if you will.

Do **not** buy the base game and all the DLC, even on sale.
Since the ocean is vast................
WONDERFUL WONDERFUL GAME!!!!! id highly recommended this game, HOWEVER, it does require a lot of storage to not be extremely laggy, so proceed with caution!!!

I pretty much never write reviews on here but it's become enough of a problem that I now want to leave a negative review. this is more about steam itself then sims 4. I am sick and tired of forced updates. I no longer wish to update the game and I don't care at all about any new features no matter how much ea wants me to I simply don't care. as I'm writing this steam just forced an update on me, going offline didn't help because the play button turned into a greyed out "update' button which i've come to read as "forced game break here" because that's all every horrible update does for me. no is no steam, stop holding my game hostage until I update it. (btw I purchased this game, I got it BEFORE it was free)

I mean, after playing this game for the first time in the year it released, all the way to the big 2026, I can't say much except, this game has taken more hours of my life than probably any other game will, and I wish my steam would be able to show all the hours I had since, but it can't

It's fine. Better off using something else rather than paying thousands for DLC. At this point I feel it would be more efficient to learn the entirety of blender to make your own models and make an entire game yourself than play this game.
I love sims but lately i havent been able to move my sims to a new lot or a plot at all developers i would love to build and not just stare expectantly at my loading screen!!!!

I can't Complain really it's the latest incarnation Of my childhood OG Sim game. if you played the sims when you were younger and haven't tried the newest one give it a go . Or even if youv'e never dipped your toe in before there's some fun to be had here, You're only limited by your imagination.
i have 1000.6 hours on this game and have spent hundreds of dollers on the sims 4 and i would do it again!
Love the building features, particulalry placing clutter.

Mein spiestand hat sich das 3 mal geloscht grad eben bin ich rein und das haus das ich gestern gebaut haber ist weg....Sims auf Pc ist einfach scheisse ich habe keine lust jedesmal ein backup zu machen nur weil die nicht fahig sind ihr spiel zu fixen dieses problem gibts schon lange. wie ware es mal das spiel zu fixen anstat uberteuerte packs rasuzubringen???? Ich habe immer gerne sims gespielt auf der alten Nintendo oder auf Ps3 und Ps4. Sims pc ist eine enttauschung.
good but the get famous pack has a bug in it when you get ready for work and need hair and makeup it doesn't work so u cant get full earnings

I used to play sims 4 on my PS4 before i got my PC and I can say that I am proud to say this is my favorite game. I use sims to make my dnd characters come to life, make my OC's more fleshed out, and to build the places i see in my mind. I wish it was compatible for Apple, but that means I just have to save up for my new laptop.
Play Paralives instead its way better and u dont have to buy dlc's ever:)
dis friggin game is pain in a** as soon as one acquires DLC which also make my PC sound like jet engine (???) and no autosave btw so there goes my 4 hour world ^_^

in the almost 350 hours ive done nothing but manage to accidentally kill every sim ive made due to grilling burgers for the 3 millionth time or listening to the radio while it was raining outside laughed so hard i farted my girlfriends pp out my butt and she had to punish me by making me recite the entire bee movie to her mother ... 10/10 life sim

This game has it's issues but I will always love playing the sims.
Drowned the entire family. Not entirely sure how I did but they are either fish or they drowned.
I just have immense amounts of fun with this game, including when it's vanilla.
Just don't waste your money on this... I suggest finding this somewhere else, so that you don't pay the 1600€ pricetag.
I can safely say this isnt worth the money youll end up spending on expansions.... also the game breaks everytime (like once a month) it gets updated so like yeah
yea, I always get lost in my Sim worlds... it's Sims, right... its the most iconic avatar-scape in gaming imo
I used to spend literal days playing sims. I cannot keep updating ts. I really hope the devs are seeing these and reverse whatever the hell they've done to this game.

Honestly, I love this game so much. I love customizing houses and getting new packs. You know what I hate though? Every single time we have to do an update, the entire game breaks for the next week. Doesn't matter if you have mods, no mods, enabled or not, it's still fixing to ruin your entire game play experience for at least the next week. I'm so tired of having to do so much extra work just to play a game. I'm exhausted. My game is stuck running, I don't know how to get it to stop being this way. It's only happening after updates. For the amount of money that we all spend on this dang game, I do expect things to work to the best of their abilities but I can't even have a comprehensive gaming experience without something stupid happening that destroys everything I did. Do freaking better.

We all know the sims and EA has gone to trash, and they don't remotely care about the community beyond us being their cash cow. Yet, warm fuzzy nostalgia, and that hope for a better tomorrow kept bringing us back. It hasn't. It's still the same crap as before, but with a side of being owned by Saudi Arabia. A regime who brutally and publicly executes people for any self expression that the government no likey. Ironic for a game built on self expression, but okay. Forget the sims, and forget EA. Be free. Have a reeses or something. Cheers.
I like that it has variety packs for different gameplays and its so fun to play for me I need to get back into playing it more because it was enjoyable!

The Sims used to be quality as a base game with fun additions through expansion packs. EA's greed has clearly gotten the better of them and they have lost their way. This iteration is boring without spending way more money to add things that were included features in previous games. The Sims has always been a series that can steal my attention for days and days, but the Sims 4 failed to do that.
